title: Rich and Adventurous Turkish Icli Kofte
description: A Mediterranean twist on a classic Turkish meatball recipe, with the addition of rich colatura and spicy bhuna. This dish is best served with a side of crusty bread and a squeeze of fresh lemon juice.

ingredients.name: Ground lamb
ingredients.notes: 80-20 lean to fat ratio works best for this recipe.

ingredients.name: Onion
ingredients.notes: Choose a sweet onion for the best flavor.

ingredients.name: Garlic
ingredients.notes: Use fresh garlic for the best flavor.

ingredients.name: Bhuna curry paste
ingredients.notes: Spicy and aromatic, bhuna adds depth to the dish.

ingredients.name: Colatura
ingredients.notes: A salty, umami-rich anchovy sauce from Italy.

ingredients.name: Breadcrumbs
ingredients.notes: Panko or plain breadcrumbs work well here.

ingredients.name: Egg
ingredients.notes: Use a room temperature egg for easier mixing.

ingredients.name: Fresh parsley
ingredients.notes: Chopped parsley adds freshness to the dish.

ingredients.name: Salt
ingredients.notes: Use kosher salt for the best flavor.

ingredients.name: Black pepper
ingredients.notes: Freshly ground black pepper enhances the flavor of the meat.

ingredients.name: Olive oil
ingredients.notes: Use a high-quality, extra virgin olive oil for the best flavor.

ingredients.name: Paella pan
ingredients.notes: A large, shallow pan with a diameter of at least 12 inches is perfect for this recipe.

ingredients.name: Tomato paste
ingredients.notes: Use a high-quality, concentrated tomato paste for the best flavor.

instructions: Mix the lamb, onion, garlic, bhuna, colatura, breadcrumbs, egg, parsley, salt, and black pepper in a large bowl until just combined.
Using your hands, shape the mixture into small, irregular balls, about 1 1/2 inches in diameter.
Heat the olive oil in a large paella pan over medium-high heat.
Add the lamb balls to the pan and cook, turning occasionally, until browned on all sides, about 5-7 minutes.
Add the tomato paste to the pan and cook, stirring constantly, for 1-2 minutes.
Reduce the heat to medium-low and simmer, covered, for 15-20 minutes, or until the lamb is cooked through and the sauce has thickened.
Serve the Icli Kofte hot, garnished with fresh parsley and a squeeze of lemon juice.
Note: Traditionally, Icli Kofte is cooked in a wood-fired oven or over an open flame. If you have access to either, feel free to use it for a more authentic flavor.
